suggested_topics: the engineering of ancient Roman aqueducts
how mycelium networks share resources underground
the economics of food trucks in major cities
the role of smell in human memory formation

formats.name: meta_swap
formats.instruction: Describe something you love as if you completely hated it (or vice versa). Use 1-2 biting sentences packed with emotion. Flip the usual tone, but don't say it's an act.

formats.name: future_flashback
formats.instruction: Write a short note as if you’re your future self, looking back at today. Paint details of both the imagined future and current moment. 80-150 words, with a timestamp.

formats.name: visual_list
formats.instruction: List 5 wildly different sensory impressions related to your topic, one per line. Each should be a standalone phrase or sentence, focusing on sound, sight, touch, taste, or smell.

formats.name: contrarian_explanation
formats.instruction: Pick a common belief about your topic and explain why it’s probably wrong. Build your argument step by step (3-5 steps), each in its own sentence. 120-200 words.

formats.name: microfiction
formats.instruction: Create a tiny story inspired by the topic. No more than 7 sentences; twist endings and oddities encouraged. Each sentence should push the plot forward.

formats.name: question_chain
formats.instruction: Begin with a central question about the topic. Follow it with three increasingly weird related questions. End with a wild hypothetical. All questions, no answers.

formats.name: supercut
formats.instruction: Rapid-fire a series of 8-10 key facts, quotes, or stats about your topic. Each point must be punchy and unrelated to the others—like a mosaic. No explanations.

formats.name: exponential_deepening
formats.instruction: Start broad with a 1-sentence overview, then repeat the topic in 4 layers: each time, shrink the scope and dive deeper, ending with a granular detail. 250-350 words.

formats.name: reverse_interview
formats.instruction: Pretend your topic is interviewing YOU. Write 5 questions it would ask, and your short responses (1-2 sentences each), focusing on surprising answers.

title_styles: Write a playful pun or wordplay title related to your topic.
Frame your title as a confession: 'I can't believe I did X for Y'.
Pose an absurd hypothetical as your title: 'What if X suddenly vanished?'
Use a mysterious, single-word title followed by a cryptic subtitle.
Give your title the tone of a headline from the year 2049.
structure_variants: Begin with a strange analogy and build your whole post around it.
Write each idea as if you're texting a best friend—casual, quick, personal.
Organize your post as a countdown (Top 5, Top 3, etc.), with a twist at #1.
Start with a completely off-topic story, then connect it to the main topic.
Use alternating paragraphs: one serious, one playful.
topic_angles: How digital habits shape physical spaces in our homes.
Secret signals people use to communicate in public.
Small tech failures that spark huge changes.
The invisible influence of packaging design on food choices.
